title: Storage Backends
description: Redis, in-memory, and Deno KV adapters

blocks.text: Hound is storage-agnostic. The db option accepts any object that implements the StorageClient interface. Three built-in adapters are provided:

rows: Local development, unit tests, single-process apps
Deno Deploy and Deno KV-based environments

blocks.text: Zero-dependency in-process storage. Data is lost on restart. Ideal for development and tests:
InMemoryStorage does not support multiple workers sharing the same queue. Use Redis for multi-process setups.
Pass any Redis-compatible client directly. Hound uses sorted sets, strings, and pub/sub — compatible with Redis 6+:
Hound is compatible with Upstash Redis (serverless) for environments where a persistent connection isn't practical.
Use the built-in Deno KV adapter for Deno Deploy or edge environments:
Implement the StorageClient interface to use any storage system. The interface mirrors the Redis command set used by Hound — zadd, zrange, get, set, del, subscribe, publish, and a handful of others.
StorageClient is exported from hound/ — import it for type-safe custom adapter development.
